factors.name: Iran-Hormuz Deal Optimism
factors.summary: Trump administration reports ceasefire extension (60 days) and Strait of Hormuz reopening in ~30 days per Nikkei/Bloomberg. Risk-on sentiment lifting equities and commodity-linked currencies; oil down ~7% to $90.32 support level, reducing input cost pressures on Dow industrials.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI crude fell ~7% to test $90/bbl on Hormuz reopening hopes. Lower energy costs support margins for Dow transportation/industrial names (BA, CAT) and reduce inflation concerns that would pressure yields.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: US Dollar retreated on Iran deal optimism (GBP/USD toward 1.3500, AUD/USD +0.70%, EUR/USD +0.37%). Weaker dollar aids Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure and reduces translation headwinds for multinational earnings.

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed 'Higher for Longer' Signal
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Treasury curve tightened to 1-year lows as traders price multiple rate-hold assumptions under new Fed chair Kevin Warsh. Prolonged higher rates weigh on dividend-yielding Dow names (utilities, consumer staples) and discount growth.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings Pipeline
factors.summary: Multiple Dow names (Elbit, Semtech, Zscaler) due to report Tuesday. Broader earnings commentary from non-Big-Tech firms suggests 'underdogs' lifting S&P 500 profit growth to 5-year highs per MarketWatch, but no specific Dow heavyweight catalysts identified in 24h window.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tail Risk (Israel-Hezbollah, Russia-Ukraine)
factors.summary: Netanyahu orders intensified Lebanon strikes despite ceasefire; Russia threatens Kyiv with 'systematic strikes.' These escalations offset Iran deal optimism and introduce gap-risk volatility that could reverse the morning's risk-on rally.

factors.name: China Macro (Tariff De-escalation)
factors.summary: CNBC notes tariff easing but trust remains low. No fresh China PMI or growth data in 24h window; Dow has heavy CAT/BA exposure to China demand, but lack of recent positive data prevents full bullish read.

factors.name: Economic Calendar (Tuesday)
factors.summary: CB Consumer Confidence data due Tuesday (light Asian calendar per investingLive). Single release insufficient to drive major Dow repricing; no ISM, inflation prints, or Fed speakers listed imminent.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h/-0.48%, 24h/-0.04%
factors.summary: Dow down -0.48% in 4h despite broad risk-on sentiment (equity futures surged). Suggests early-session gains reversed; short-term momentum stalling even as macro backdrop improves.

key_levels.support: 50,400–50,500 (4h lows, trend line from late Feb–Apr lows in crude analogy)

screening.narrative: Iran-Hormuz deal momentum dominated Monday open: Trump signaled ceasefire extension and Hormuz reopening ~30 days, oil crashed ~7% to $90 support, USD weakened sharply, and risk sentiment surged globally. However, the Dow itself is treading water (-0.48% in 4h), suggesting profit-taking or rotation away from cyclicals into rate-sensitive segments. Overlay of Fed 'higher-for-longer' chatter (Warsh) and escalating Israel-Hezbollah/Russia-Ukraine hostilities are capping upside enthusiasm. The move is in progress but losing steam into US cash open.
catalysts_ahead: CB Consumer Confidence (Tuesday, May 26) — watch for demand signals relevant to Dow consumer names
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (early June) — direct read on Dow industrial composition
Dow heavyweight earnings (ongoing: UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, BA — price-weighted impact high)
Fed chair Kevin Warsh commentary (implies higher-for-longer bias; next FOMC TBD)
Oil inventory data (API, EIA) — tracks Hormuz reopening speed and CVX/XOM narrative
China economic data (June window) — CAT, BA, materials demand critical to Dow valuation
Further Israel-Hezbollah or Russia-Ukraine escalation (real-time tail risk)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal could stall or reverse if military escalation resumes (reports of US strikes on Iranian missile sites already Monday suggest fragility). Stronger-than-expected inflation or hawkish Fed speaker could reverse the yield-friendly backdrop. Dow's international revenue exposure leaves it vulnerable to renewed geopolitical shock or yuan weakness if China slowdown accelerates. If Hormuz remains closed longer than 30 days, oil rallies sharply and margins for industrials compress.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 (in progress, 4h momentum weakening despite positive macro). Wait for intraday retest of support 50,400–50,500 or confirmation of higher-close into US cash session before adding long exposure. Alternatively, short any pop toward 50,750 resistance if price action fails to sustain above pre-weekend highs.

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on southern Iran (missile sites, boats) at 2026-05-26 00:57 UTC clouded peace deal outlook despite simultaneous Qatar negotiations. Market volatility elevated; Rubio says deal language 'could take days' (FXStreet 2026-05-26T04:00).

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility
factors.summary: Crude oil futures plunged ~5% (WTI near $91.79, down 4.98%) on ceasefire optimism, but rebounded slightly after US strikes (WTI ~$90.60 per FXStreet 01:16). Dow has ~1.5% energy exposure (CVX); volatile energy costs pressurize industrial input costs.

factors.name: USD Strength & Safe-Haven Demand
factors.summary: DXY holding above 99.00 (FXStreet 02:29) on renewed geopolitical uncertainty and Strait of Hormuz risk. Stronger dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue; inverse effect on multinational earnings translation.

factors.name: Risk-Off Sentiment in Equities
factors.summary: AUD/USD, NZD/USD, GBP/USD all declining on risk aversion (FXStreet 01:16, 00:47, 01:15). Asian markets mixed despite KOSPI hitting record high (All News 04:11). Dow futures implied weakness from oil strike shock offsetting earlier Iran deal optimism.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings Pipeline
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings reported in news window. Healthcare (UNH) sector discussion (Bloomberg 04:52) but no earnings catalyst. Earnings visibility low over next 48h.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No new Fed commentary or FOMC announcements in news. BOJ Deputy Gov Himino reaffirmed rate hike path contingent on Middle East developments (FXStreet 01:36). No imminent US rate shock signaled.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yields
factors.summary: Treasuries rallied 'across the curve' post-holiday on Iran deal optimism (Bloomberg 00:06), but yields not explicitly quoted. BOJ noting 'global inflation worries' reflected in long-end yields (FXStreet 01:44); no fresh yield data provided.

factors.name: Inflation/CPI/PPI Data
factors.summary: No new inflation readings in news window. Sri Lanka's 100-bp emergency rate hike cited 'surging oil prices from Middle East tensions' pushing April CPI to 5.4% (investingLive 02:18). No US CPI/PPI surprise data.

factors.name: China Economic Data & PMI
factors.summary: PBOC set USD/CNY at 6.8288 vs estimate 6.7822 (FXStreet 01:15), signaling yuan softness amid capital outflow concerns. China tightening overseas stock trading controls (Bloomberg 02:58). No PMI or growth data released.

factors.name: Commodity Metals & Industrial Input Costs
factors.summary: Copper holding gains on Iran deal hopes (Bloomberg 03:13). Wheat extending longest losing streak since January on ceasefire optimism (Bloomberg 02:34). Chinese coking coal prices rising post-Shanxi mine disaster (Bloomberg 03:02). No consistent pressure signal.

factors.name: Consumer Confidence & Retail Data
factors.summary: No US consumer confidence, retail sales, or housing data in news window. Starbucks Korea sales fell sharply on 'Tank Day' ad backlash (Al Jazeera 04:38), but isolated to one brand/region.

factors.name: VIX & Credit Spreads
factors.summary: VIX not explicitly quoted in news. Implied volatility elevated on Iran strikes; no credit spread widening data provided. ASX fell 10%+ on tech capex concerns (Stock Market News 01:34), isolated to exchange operator.

factors.name: Trade Policy & Tariffs
factors.summary: Canada-India free trade talks progressing (investingLive 00:39). China tariff easing noted but 'trust didn't' per CNBC (investingLive 22:58). Trump 'softening nuclear stance' on Iran deal (investingLive 22:08). No new US tariff shocks.

screening.narrative: US30 is caught in competing narratives: optimism over a potential US-Iran ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening (lifting energy prices and risk appetite early) collided with fresh US military strikes that cloud deal prospects and resurrect geopolitical premium. The 4h decline of -0.48% and minimal 24h move (-0.04%) reflect choppy consolidation as traders digest simultaneous deflationary (ceasefire = lower oil) and deflationary-hedging (strike risk = safe-haven dollar) signals. Dow's heavy international exposure and energy sector weight add downside bias to USD strength and oil volatility, but no fresh data shock (CPI, Fed, earnings) anchors conviction. Market is in PHASE 2 chop: directional move not fully underway, awaiting either a credible Iran deal breakthrough or escalation confirmation.
catalysts_ahead: Bank of Korea rate decision (May 28, 2026) — expected hold at 2.50%, but 70% of economists see Q3 hikes on oil-driven inflation (investingLive 00:57)
US-Iran deal language finalization — Rubio indicates several more days (FXStreet 04:00); any breakthrough or collapse would flip risk sentiment sharply
Fed policy communication — no FOMC scheduled imminent, but BOJ/ECB messaging on rates ongoing; BOJ rate hike path under Middle East watch
Oil inventory data (API/EIA) — would clarify supply/demand post-Hormuz outlook
Dow component earnings guidance — no major releases announced in window; watch for forward guidance on tariff/energy costs
China macro data — no PMI or growth releases in immediate 24-48h, but yuan weakness and capital controls signal economic caution
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) credible, binding Iran ceasefire agreement announced → oil collapse, risk-on rally, dollar weakness flip Dow to sustained upside; (2) Middle East escalation / Hormuz closure → oil spike above $105, equity selloff, flight-to-quality hammers Dow cyclicals; (3) surprise hawkish Fed/BOJ messaging → rate spike, dividend payer repricing; (4) strong US earnings beat offsetting geopolitical noise → Dow component strength absorbs macro chop.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is choppy and extended in neither direction (4h: -0.48%, 24h: -0.04%); avoid chasing. On retest of 50,400 support with Iran deal headlines stabilizing, consider small long probe. On break below 50,400 with fresh escalation, 50,300 is next target. Wait for either deal announcement or end-of-day consolidation before committing size; event risk is too elevated for conviction entries.

factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iranian missile sites and boats (BBC, 09:44 UTC) have escalated tensions despite ongoing peace talks in Qatar. Iran's IRGC downed an MQ-9 drone and signaled willingness to respond, introducing binary outcome risk: deal breakthrough or further escalation. Initial euphoria over peace talks (futures +0.53% at 08:41 UTC) has been partially offset by strike uncertainty.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility
factors.summary: WTI crude rebounded from $88.70 two-week low to near $93 but remains highly sensitive to Strait of Hormuz closure risk. Brent at 98.1 USD/bbl (below Friday's 103.5 close per Danske Bank). Elevated energy input costs pressure Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, BA) and consumer discretionary; China's strategic reserves release (MarketWatch, 09:42) suppresses upside.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield fell >6 bps to 4.510% post-Memorial Day (MarketWatch, 09:19), reversing prior week's uptrend. ECB signaling June hike (ECB member Lane, 09:03 UTC) and hawkish tilt, but Fed hawkish risks remain per BNY (06:54 UTC). Lower yields support equity multiples, but geopolitical premium is keeping upside capped.

factors.name: US Dollar & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD stabilized above 99.00 DXY early European session (06:59 UTC) after initial weakness on Iran deal optimism. Dollar strength relative to EM (INR, NZD) persists, but ~50% Dow international revenue exposure means translation headwinds remain if USD holds. No clear directional conviction in FX.

factors.name: Equity Futures Momentum
factors.summary: Dow futures +0.53%, S&P 500 futures +0.54%, Nasdaq 100 futures +0.73% at 08:41 UTC amid peace deal hopes and falling oil. However, European equities snapped six-day win streak (Bloomberg, 07:26), signaling profit-taking and deal-doubt creeping in. Move is intraday, not extended yet.

factors.name: Cyclical & Industrial Earnings Outlook
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings released in 24h window. Forward guidance themes obscured by geopolitical noise. Defense/industrial names (Elbit Systems +15% Q1 revenue, All News 09:31) see tailwinds, but macro uncertainty from Middle East conflict and China slowdown (policy rates at record low per Bloomberg 07:03) cloud broader industrial demand.

factors.name: China Economic Support
factors.summary: China cut one-year policy loan rate to record low (Bloomberg, 07:03 UTC), signaling stimulus focus. However, delayed effect on Dow-heavy exporters (CAT, BA, GE). Mixed signal: policy accommodation vs. underlying growth weakness concerns.

factors.name: Consumer & Cyclical Demand Signals
factors.summary: No major US consumer data (retail sales, housing starts, ISM) released in 24h. Japan economy retains 'moderate recovery' assessment but notes Middle East headwinds (Investinglive, 09:19 UTC). Absence of negative surprises is mildly supportive, but lack of positive catalysts limits upside.

factors.name: Volatility & Geopolitical Premium
factors.summary: VIX and credit spreads not explicitly quoted, but news flow emphasizes binary Iran outcome risk and Ukraine tensions (Russia warns of 'systematic' Kyiv strikes, Al Jazeera 08:11). Geopolitical premium is likely elevated, capping risk appetite.

factors.name: Market Sentiment & Profit-Taking
factors.summary: S&P 500 logged 8th consecutive weekly gain (Deutsche Bank, 07:49 UTC); equities rallied into 4h window on hope, but European close saw reversal. Typical extended-move exhaustion pattern. Dow -0.48% in 4h suggests local resistance being tested.

key_levels.support: 50,350–50,400 (4h: test of overnight low near session open)

screening.narrative: The Dow opened the US session with modest tailwinds from peace-deal optimism (Iran talks in Qatar, futures +0.53%), lower Treasury yields (10Y down 6 bps to 4.51%), and falling oil (WTI near $93). However, fresh US military strikes on Iranian missile sites and boats, combined with Iran's IRGC downing a US drone, have introduced acute binary risk. While equity futures remain modestly positive, the current -0.48% 4h pullback and European equities' break of a 6-day win streak signal profit-taking and elevated caution into this uncertain outcome. The move has NOT extended yet (still intraday, <1% drawdown), but sentiment is fragile.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran negotiations outcome (real-time, Qatar talks ongoing)
Strait of Hormuz reopening status (affects oil, energy input costs)
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (week ahead, critical for Dow industrials)
Durable goods orders, factory orders (weekly data, forward industrial demand)
ECB June rate decision (hawkish tilt confirmed; timing unclear in 24-72h window)
Fed Chair Powell or other FOMC comments (hawkish vs. dovish surprise risk)
Dow component earnings: UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA (staggered, some may report this week)
China PMI releases (gauge stimulus impact on Dow exporters)
Ukrainian conflict developments (systematic Russia strikes on Kyiv threat)
screening.risk_warning: A breakthrough US-Iran deal announcement would likely trigger sharp equity rally (+0.5–1.0%) and oil drop, invalidating the cautious bias. Conversely, escalation (Iran retaliatory strikes on US bases or Israeli targets) would break support and drive Dow lower. Absence of clear catalysts in next 24-48h suggests continued choppy consolidation; overconfidence in either direction is premature.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early intraday consolidation (4h: -0.48%, not extended). Wait for either a clear breakout above 50,650 on deal news, or a retest and hold of 50,350 support before re-entering long. Short-term traders: avoid chasing; next 2–4 hours likely to chop into Iran headline risk. Bias remains NEUTRAL until geopolitical outcome clarifies or major economic data arrives.

factors.name: Price Action & Phase
factors.summary: Dow down only -0.22% in both 4h and 24h windows; minimal directional conviction. Prior bias history (BUY→NEUTRAL→NEUTRAL) shows indecision, not sustained directional pressure.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Citadel Securities warns Fed is 'falling behind curve' and should hike; TD Securities expects Fed to drop easing bias in June and hold through 2026 with cuts delayed to 2027. Higher-for-longer stance pressures equity multiples.

factors.name: Consumer Confidence
factors.summary: Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index declined to 93.1 in May from 93.8 in April; Present Situation Index fell 3.2 points to 121.2, signaling weakening current economic views amid rising prices.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk & Oil Prices
factors.summary: Fresh US-Iran military strikes escalate despite peace talks; Brent oil jumps above $100/bbl; Israel operations in southern Lebanon; stagflation risk cited by TD Securities (high oil, supply chain stress, persistent inflation).

factors.name: Manufacturing & Dallas Fed
factors.summary: Dallas Fed Manufacturing index improved to 0.40 in May from -2.30 in April, signaling modest stabilization. Production index at 9.4 still shows expansion, but capacity utilization and new orders indices remain weak.

factors.name: Semiconductor & Tech Sector Rally
factors.summary: Micron surges 17.16% and hits $1T market cap for first time; broad chip rally (AMD +5.68%, AVGO +4.84%, INTC +1.72%) on AI memory demand; SpaceX IPO enthusiasm lifts space stocks. Dow exposure to these themes is moderate.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings & M&A
factors.summary: Eli Lilly announces ~$3.8B in vaccine acquisitions and gains on expansion strategy; Micron benefits from AI-driven chip shortage. No major negative earnings surprises reported in 24h window; insufficient data to drive conviction.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD supported by hawkish Fed narrative and safe-haven flows from Iran escalation; EUR/USD at 1.1622 (-0.15% session). Geopolitical premium temporarily supports USD, but speculative positioning turned net short after initial safe-haven move (Rabobank).

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Rate-Sensitive Names
factors.summary: Higher-for-longer Fed stance and inflation persistence push real yields higher, pressuring dividend payers and financials. No specific 10-year yield data in latest news, but narrative favors hold/hike not cuts.

factors.name: Trade & Tariff Risk
factors.summary: Trump to host Cabinet meeting at Camp David on Iran talks; no new trade/tariff announcements in 24h window. Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure remains exposed to policy shifts but no immediate catalyst.

key_levels.resistance: 50760 (early-session high mentioned; Dow closed record 50,759 on Friday)

screening.narrative: Dow is navigating cross-currents: hawkish Fed repricing and consumer confidence deterioration weigh against scattered bullish signals from semiconductors, Lilly M&A, and Dallas Fed stabilization. Iran escalation and oil surge above $100/bbl introduce stagflation risks, while consumer sentiment erosion threatens demand. Lack of directional price action (-0.22%) and transition from prior BUY to NEUTRAL bias suggest market is digesting conflicting signals heading into week's end.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC minutes or Fed speaker commentary (rate-cut repricing)
Upcoming ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (critical for Dow industrials)
Durable goods orders, factory orders (cyclical Dow exposure)
Further Iran ceasefire developments or oil price volatility
Dow component earnings season (UNH, JPM, GS, BA guidance)
Treasury auction or yield curve inversion signals
China PMI/GDP if released (Caterpillar, Boeing demand signal)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Oil retreats sharply on Iran deal breakthrough, lifting consumer confidence and reducing stagflation fears → BULLISH; (2) Fed speakers deliver dovish surprise or inflation data cools, prompting rate-cut repricing → BULLISH; (3) Semiconductor rally broadens to transport & industrials, offsetting consumer weakness → BULLISH. Conversely, (4) further geopolitical escalation or supply-chain disruption could accelerate bearish move if confidence falls below 90.
screening.entry_guidance: Insufficient directional conviction to initiate new positions. If bearish bias firms on consumer data or Fed hawkishness, consider short entry on retest of 50,700 resistance. If geopolitical fears ease and oil breaks below $98, watch for bullish reversal off 50,200 support. Default: wait for >0.5% directional break and 2-3 bar confirmation before committing capital.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Kashkari (Minneapolis Fed) signals potential rate hike series if Middle East inflation pressures persist. Treasury 2Y auction at 4.071% shows sticky rates. Higher-for-longer stance pressures rate-sensitive Dow names (financials, utilities).

factors.name: Oil & Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: Brent crude up >3% post-Iran retaliation vow. US strikes in southern Iran mid-ceasefire talks create 'Hormuz closure' tail risk (SocGen stress case $200/bbl). Oil input costs will compress margins for industrial Dow heavies (CAT, BA, MMM).

factors.name: Real Yields & Bond Selloff
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'Bond Selloff Is About More Than Inflation'—higher real rate regime emerging. Long-end pressure weighs on dividend-heavy Dow composition (utilities, staples). Inflation expectations climbing while Fed tightens.

factors.name: AI/Semiconductor Euphoria (Specific to Index Composition)
factors.summary: Micron (MU) +20.8% today, +860% YoY; hits $1T market cap. Broader 'AI optimism' lifts S&P 500 and Nasdaq. However, Micron is NOT in Dow 30 (price-weighted); limited direct benefit. Positive sentiment backdrop for growth exposure.

factors.name: USD Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD/JPY breaks 159.50, Gold dives >1.6% below $4,500 on USD haven demand post-Iran strikes. Strong dollar headwind for ~50% of Dow revenue (CAT, BA, MSFT, JNJ, V overseas earnings translate lower).

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment & Macro Confidence
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports 'Americans feel worse than ever' per consumer survey; White House disputes data. Tensions between admin messaging and on-ground sentiment. Risk to discretionary Dow names (HD, MCD, NKE indirect exposure via retail).

factors.name: Industrial/Cyclical Weakness Signals
factors.summary: AutoZone down sharply (worst day since Mar 2020) on margin compression, inflation, energy costs, supply-chain disruption fears. Broader signal for margin pressure on industrial Dow names (CAT, BA, HON). DuPont earnings tonight; oil price shock material.

factors.name: Earnings & Forward Guidance Pipeline
factors.summary: Eli Lilly vaccine deal ($3.8B) shows M&A confidence; Edwards Lifesciences hits 52-week high. Mixed signals—some names rallying, others (AutoZone, Ferrari) faltering. Awaiting CAT, BA, GE guidance on capex/demand.

factors.name: China Growth & Global Trade
factors.summary: No major China PMI or economic data in news window. Trade policy uncertainty lingers (Trump administration stance). Tariff/geopolitical risk elevated but not crystallized yet in Dow positioning.

factors.name: Technicals & Price Action
factors.summary: 4h change -0.20%, 24h change -0.35% — flat, consolidative. Bias history shows three consecutive NEUTRAL5/10 reads (May 26 05:03, 09:53, 16:42). No directional conviction yet; waiting for breakout catalyst.

screening.narrative: The Dow faces a tug-of-war between AI-fueled sentiment (Micron's AI chip boom benefiting broad equities) and mounting headwinds from Fed tightening, oil shocks, and USD strength. Kashkari's hawkish pivot on inflation + escalating Iran tensions (Hormuz closure risk, Brent >3% higher) are pushing real yields up and pressuring multinational translation (strong dollar). Consumer mood cracks, AutoZone margin pressure, and sticky Treasury rates all signal cyclical slowdown ahead. AI enthusiasm is real but largely confined to mega-cap tech *outside* the Dow (NVDA, TSLA, MU). The index remains flat pending clarity on Fed trajectory and geopolitical resolution.
catalysts_ahead: DuPont earnings tonight (oil price shock narrative critical for Dow industrials)
Australian CPI release (real yields/AUD/USD direction)
US-Iran ceasefire status / Hormuz shipping flows (oil volatility driver)
Fed speakers (Kashkari, other FOMC members) on rate-hike timing
ISM Services PMI (early June) — key Dow composition demand signal
Treasury auctions (2Y, 5Y, 10Y) — real yield regime lock-in
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US ceasefire holds and oil tumbles >$5/bbl, inflation fears recede and Fed pivots dovish—Dow rallies on lower real yields and dollar weakness. Conversely, if Hormuz closes, oil spikes >$100/bbl sustained, and CPI re-accelerates, Dow faces sharp selloff on stagflation + margin compression. Micron's AI euphoria spreading to traditional cyclicals (CAT, BA, GE) would flip bias bullish but lacks current confirmation.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is flat and unconvincing (PHASE_1). Avoid chasing here. Wait for break above 50,600 resistance (if Fed doves or Hormuz risk fades) OR drop below 50,250 support (if oil spikes or margin warnings intensify). Given geopolitical uncertainty and earnings disappointments (AutoZone), bias toward short entries on any relief rally into 50,500–50,600, targeting 50,300 support.
